title: Tender French Duck Confit
description: Sun-dried French duck leg, rich with sunflower oil and caramel flavors, perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together salt, black pepper, and cinnamon.
Rub the spice mixture all over the duck legs, making sure to coat them evenly.
Heat the sunflower oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the duck from the pot and add the garlic, cooking until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the red wine to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the duck to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the duck for 2 hours, or until it's tender and the meat is falling off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the fat from the pot and discard the solids.
Serve the duck legs with the remaining fat spooned over the top.
